findings.remediation: Treat the server as untrusted; the model may obey embedded instructions.

description:
Create a new trigger for an AI agent.

Triggers determine when the agent activates.

Trigger types:
- incoming_message: Activates on new incoming messages
- schedule: Activates on a schedule
- webhook: Activates on webhook events
- event: Activates on system events

labels.name: agents_trigger_update
labels.description:
Update an existing AI agent trigger.

All parameters are optional — only provided fields will be updated.

labels.name: agents_trigger_delete
labels.description:
Delete a trigger from an AI agent.

WARNING: This cannot be undone.

labels.name: webhooks_configure_enrichment
labels.description:
Enable enrichment on an agent's webhook trigger so reference-only webhook payloads are resolved to full data before the agent runs. One tool for every provider — choose `provider` and pass its `config`.

Providers & required config:
- meta_leadgen (Meta/Facebook Lead Ads): config = {app_id, app_secr

labels.name: agents_list_drafts
labels.description:
List pending agent drafts awaiting approval.

Shows drafts that have been generated by AI agents but not yet sent.
Each draft includes:
- Thread/conversation info
- Trigger message (what prompted the reply)
- Generated response text
- Creation time and expiration

Use this when user asks:
- 'Show pe

labels.name: agents_approve_draft
labels.description:
Approve a pending agent draft and send the message.

The draft will be sent to the conversation it was generated for.
You can optionally edit the text before sending.

Use this when user says:
- 'Approve this draft'
- 'Send this reply'
- 'Approve and send'
- 'Looks good, send it'

IMPORTANT: This wi

labels.name: agents_reject_draft
labels.description:
Reject a pending agent draft without sending.

The draft will be marked as rejected and won't be sent.
Use this when the generated response isn't appropriate.

Use this when user says:
- 'Reject this draft'
- 'Don't send this'
- 'Cancel this reply'
- 'Delete this draft'
- 'This response is wrong'

labels.name: agents_list_files
labels.description:
List files directly attached to this agent (agent-specific files, not shared collections).

Returns file_id, title, status, and chunk_count for each file.
chunk_count shows how many indexed chunks were created — 0 means the file is still processing.

Use agents.add_file to attach a new file, or agen

labels.name: agents_add_file
labels.description:
Attach a file to this agent's private knowledge (agent-specific files, not shared with other agents).

Workflow:
1. Upload the file with files_upload (pass source_url for remote files)
2. Index it with files_ingest (pass the file_id)
3. Call this tool with agent_id + file_id

Returns chunk_count — s

labels.name: agents_remove_file
labels.description:
Remove a file from this agent's private knowledge.

The file itself is not deleted — it's just detached from this agent.
Use agents.list_files to find the file_id to remove.

labels.name: agents_task_complete
labels.description: Report that a Claude Code agent task has been completed. Call this when you finish processing an agent_task from DialogBrain.

labels.name: agent_handoff
labels.description: Delegate a multi-step task (research, composing messages, booking, scheduling) to the full agentic planner. Use when a user ask needs more than a direct answer. The specialist runs synchronously — its response is already shown to the user in real-time. Summarize the OUTCOME in past tense (e.g. 'The

labels.name: agent_silence
labels.description: End this turn without sending any message. Use when the thread is owned by a human operator after job.escalate, when the guest is self-resolving, when the message is a duplicate, or for observation-only turns. Calling this tool is the ONLY correct way to stay silent — narrated silence text (e.g. '*(

labels.name: agents_traces_list
labels.description:
List recent execution traces for an agent — the same data as /admin/requests, scoped to one agent and readable by an LLM.

Use this when an agent call timed out, drafted the wrong response, or you want to know which tool/LLM call burned the latency. Pair with `agents.trace_get` for full detail on a

labels.name: agents_trace_get
labels.description:
Fetch the full execution detail for a single trace — tool executions, events timeline, LLM call spans (with error_message on failures).

Use after `agents.traces_list` identifies a specific trace of interest (failed run, slow run, unexpected outcome).

By default LLM `system_prompt` and `prompt_mess

labels.name: agents_traces_stats
labels.description:
Aggregated trace statistics for one agent over the last N days — total runs, success rate, avg duration, error breakdown, top tools used, runs-per-day histogram.

Use this when you want a bird's-eye view of an agent's health before diving into individual traces with `agents.traces_list` / `agents.tr

labels.name: agents_activity
labels.description: See what you — or another agent in your workspace — actually did over a time window: messages sent, documents created, calls made, plus a summary (run counts, per-day, top tools). Use this to answer 'what did I do today / yesterday / last week / in the last hour?' or 'what did <agent> do?' with real

labels.name: agents_prompt_history
labels.description: List past versions of an agent's `prompt_text`. Every edit to the agent's prompt is snapshotted to an append-only table — use this tool to browse history, find a prior known-good version, and copy it into `agents.prompt_restore`.

labels.name: agents_prompt_restore
labels.description: Restore a past version of an agent's `prompt_text` by version_number. Creates a new version pointing at the restored content — history is preserved. Use `agents.prompt_history` first to find the version_number you want.

labels.name: agents_simulate_inbound
labels.description: Replay an inbound message on a thread through the real trigger pipeline and return what would have happened. The router auto-picks the winning enabled agent + trigger by priority/specificity (same logic as production). By default send_mode='draft' so no real message is sent; pass send_mode='auto' on

labels.name: agents_update_from_template
labels.description:
Update a forked agent's instructions (prompt) to the latest version of the system template it was created from.

Use when the platform has improved a template and the user wants their forked agent to pick up the new prompt. This OVERWRITES the agent's prompt_text with the template's current prompt —

labels.name: agents_set_integration
labels.description:
Enable or disable a connected workspace integration on an AI agent — this controls which ext<id>_<name> integration tools the agent (and its sandboxed workbench runs) may call. Use integrations_list to get the workspace_integration_id.

When enabling with no explicit denied_tools, WRITE-class tools

labels.name: agents_list_integrations
labels.description: List the workspace integrations enabled (or available) for an AI agent, with each one's workspace_integration_id, provider, enabled flag, and count of denied tools. Use this to see what an agent can call before toggling with agents_set_integration.

labels.name: collections_list
labels.description:
List all knowledge collections in the workspace.

Collections are named groups of files used for RAG search. Auto-created collections (per-agent, per-prompt) are hidden by default.

labels.name: collections_create
labels.description:
Create a named knowledge collection.

Collections group files for RAG search. After creating, add files with collections.add_file and assign to agents with collections.assign_agent.

labels.name: collections_delete
labels.description:
Delete a knowledge collection.

If the collection is assigned to agents, prompts, or channels, pass force=true to delete anyway. CASCADE removes all assignments automatically.

labels.name: collections_add_file
labels.description:
Add a file to a knowledge collection.

The file must be uploaded and indexed first (files_upload + files_ingest). If the file was previously removed, it is re-enabled.

labels.name: collections_remove_file
labels.description:
Remove a file from a knowledge collection.

The file itself is not deleted — only the collection membership is removed.

labels.name: collections_list_files
labels.description: List all files in a knowledge collection with their indexing status and chunk counts. Each returned file has a `file_id` (integer) that can be passed to messages.send as `attachments=[file_id]` to send the file to a contact, or to files.read to read its text content.

labels.name: collections_assign_agent
labels.description:
Assign a knowledge collection to an AI agent.

Once assigned, the agent's knowledge.query will automatically scope RAG search to files in its assigned collections.

labels.name: collections_unassign_agent
labels.description:
Remove a knowledge collection from an AI agent.

The collection and its files are not deleted — only the agent assignment is removed.

labels.name: prompts_list
labels.description:
List all prompt templates in this workspace.

Returns id + name + description + category so you know which prompt_id to use in prompts.get or prompts.update.

labels.name: prompts_get
labels.description:
Get full content of a prompt template: system instructions (prompt_text) and auto-reply rules.

Run prompts.list first to find the prompt_id.

labels.name: prompts_update
labels.description:
Update a prompt template's name, system instructions, or auto-reply rules.

Changes affect every agent using this template, unless the agent has its own override (set via agents.update → prompt_text).

All parameters except prompt_id are optional — only provided fields are updated.

labels.name: prompts_prompt_history
labels.description: List past versions of a prompt template's `prompt_text`. Every edit is snapshotted to an append-only table — use this to browse history and find a version_number for `prompts.prompt_restore`.

labels.name: prompts_prompt_restore
labels.description: Restore a past version of a prompt template by version_number. Creates a new version pointing at the restored content — history is preserved. Fans out to every agent using this template without a per-agent override; the response includes `affected_agents` as a receipt of the fan-out.

labels.name: widgets_list
labels.description:
List all livechat widgets.

Returns widgets with their configuration, embed code, and status.

Use this when user wants to see their widgets or chat widgets.

labels.name: widgets_create
labels.description:
Create a new livechat widget for your website.

The widget will be created with default settings.
You can customize theme, auto-reply mode, and more.

Use this when user wants to add a chat widget to their site.

labels.name: widgets_update
labels.description:
Update an existing livechat widget configuration.

You can change name, theme, auto-reply mode, and other settings.
Only provided fields will be updated.

Use this when user wants to modify their chat widget settings.

labels.name: widgets_delete
labels.description:
Delete a livechat widget permanently.

This will remove the widget and its embed code will stop working.
Existing chat history will be preserved.

Use this when user wants to remove a chat widget.

labels.name: widgets_get_embed_code
labels.description:
Get the embed code snippet for a livechat widget.

Returns HTML/JavaScript code to add to your website.
The code should be placed before the closing </body> tag.

Use this when user wants to install the chat widget on their site.

labels.name: widgets_get
labels.description:
Get full configuration of a single livechat widget.

Returns all settings including theme, identification, actions, and more.

Use this when user wants to see or verify a specific widget's settings.

labels.name: linkedin_search
labels.description: Search LinkedIn for people, companies, jobs, or posts. Supports filtering by keywords, location, industry, network distance, and more. Use linkedin.search_filters first to resolve filter keywords to LinkedIn parameter IDs.

labels.name: linkedin_search_filters
labels.description: Get LinkedIn search filter parameter IDs. LinkedIn uses internal IDs instead of text for search filters (location, industry, etc.). Call this before linkedin.search to resolve filter keywords to their LinkedIn parameter IDs.

labels.name: linkedin_get_profile
labels.description: Get a LinkedIn user profile by ID, public identifier (vanity name), or profile URL. Returns name, headline, location, and other profile information.

labels.name: linkedin_get_company
labels.description: Get a LinkedIn company profile by company ID or vanity name. Returns company name, description, industry, size, and other details.

labels.name: linkedin_update_profile
labels.description: Update the authenticated user's own LinkedIn profile. Supports adding/editing experience entries (role, company, skills, dates). Also supports updating location. Headline, summary, education are NOT supported by the API.

labels.name: linkedin_invite
labels.description: Send a connection invitation to a LinkedIn user. Optionally include a personalized message (max 300 characters). Rate limited: LinkedIn allows 80-100 invitations per day, max 200 per week.

labels.name: linkedin_list_connections
labels.description: List your LinkedIn connections, sorted by most recently added.

labels.name: linkedin_list_invitations_sent
labels.description: List your pending sent connection invitations on LinkedIn.

labels.name: linkedin_list_reactions
labels.description: List all reactions (likes, celebrates, etc.) on a specific LinkedIn post.

labels.name: linkedin_add_comment
labels.description: Add a comment to a LinkedIn post. Use post_id from search results or thread data.

labels.name: linkedin_raw_request
labels.description: Send an arbitrary LinkedIn API request via Unipile's magic route. Only GET and POST methods are allowed. WARNING: This bypasses structured rate limiting and can perform destructive actions. Use this only when no other LinkedIn tool covers the needed functionality.

labels.name: search_messages
labels.description: Search message content across all chats — semantic + keyword. Use to find what was said: quotes, topics, info exchanged. For chats/threads themselves use search.threads; for files use search.files; for links use search.links.

labels.name: search_threads
labels.description: Find or list chat threads/conversations — by topic, participant, unread/unanswered status, or recency. Omit `query` to list threads by filter. For message content use search.messages; for files use search.files. `since` filters by recency and pairs with only_unread / only_unanswered.

labels.name: search_files
labels.description: Search files and attachments across the workspace — by content, filename, document type, or origin. For message content use search.messages; for links use search.links.

labels.name: search_links
labels.description: Search links/URLs shared across the workspace — by type, owner, or associated contact. For files use search.files; for message content use search.messages.

labels.name: workspace_list
labels.description: List every workspace the caller is a member of, with `is_current` marking the workspace this MCP key is currently routed to. Pair with `workspace.switch` to change the active workspace without reconnecting.

labels.name: workspace_current
labels.description: Return the workspace this MCP API key is currently routed to, with the caller's role inside it. Use this to confirm context before/after `workspace.switch`.

labels.name: workspace_switch
labels.description: Re-point the active MCP API key to a different workspace. Pass exactly one of `workspace_id` or `slug` (find them via `workspace.list`). Takes effect on the very next tool call — no MCP reconnect, no new API key. Sequential checkpoint: do not parallelize tool calls across a switch — calls already in

labels.name: integrations_list
labels.description: List the workspace's external integrations (HTTP-API + MCP), each with its id, provider name, base_url, transport, status, and registered tool count. Use before integrations.add_endpoints to find the integration id / base_url.

labels.name: integrations_get_endpoints
labels.description: List every endpoint (operationId, method, path) registered on an HTTP-API integration. Identify it by `integration_id` or `base_url`. Use this to review what tools exist before integrations.remove_endpoints or add_endpoints.

labels.name: integrations_add_endpoints
labels.description: Add one or more API endpoints to an HTTP-API integration as callable tools, merged additively into the integration for `base_url` (created if none exists). Each endpoint becomes a tool with params + request/response schemas inferred from the samples you pass. When CREATING a new integration, provide

labels.name: integrations_remove_endpoints
labels.description: Remove endpoints (tools) from an HTTP-API integration — e.g. junk paths like static assets, /socket.io, or SPA routes that aren't real API calls. Identify the integration by `integration_id` or `base_url`, and the endpoints to drop by `operation_ids` (e.g. getSocketIo) and/or `paths` (e.g. /socket.i

labels.name: integrations_set_auth
labels.description: Set or update the authentication on an HTTP-API integration, generically. Identify it by `integration_id` or `base_url`. Pass `auth` = {type: bearer|api_key|basic|custom_headers|browser_identity|none, ...}: bearer/basic → {token}; api_key → {token, header_name?}; custom_headers → {headers:{name:valu

labels.name: integrations_capture_session
labels.description: Restore an expired integration session by capturing the LIVE auth of an open browser page — works for COOKIE sessions (browser_identity) AND TOKEN/HEADER sessions (bearer / api_key / custom_headers, e.g. Devise access-token/client/uid). First call browser_open(url, identity_name) for the integration

labels.name: job_read_context
labels.description: Read the current job context. Returns the full state of your active job including assignments, escalations, and any data you previously stored.

labels.name: job_update_context
labels.description: Update the job context by merging new data. Existing keys are preserved unless explicitly overwritten. Use this to record progress, update assignment statuses, or store intermediate results.

labels.name: job_complete
labels.description: Mark the job as completed. This sanitizes PII from the context and records a completion summary. Use when all tasks in the job are done.

labels.name: job_escalate
labels.description: Escalate the job to a human. Use when you cannot resolve an issue, someone is not responding, or a situation requires human judgment.

labels.name: notes_save
labels.description: Save a fact or note into the agent's memory. Use scope to choose visibility: 'workspace' = visible to every agent in this workspace (use for shared facts, project conventions); 'agent' = private to this agent (use for personal working notes); 'thread' = scoped to one conversation (use for thread-spe

labels.name: notes_recall
labels.description: Recall notes from your notebook. By default returns only your own notes (all scopes, newest first). Pass filter_agent_id=<int> to read another agent's notebook, or filter_agent_id="all" (or "*") to read across every agent in the workspace. Pass scope to narrow to global/thread/person. Each result in

labels.name: notes_search
labels.description: Full-text search in your notebook. By default searches only your own notes. Pass filter_agent_id=<int> to search another agent's notebook, or "all" (or "*") for workspace-wide. Or list all notes for a person/thread by scope_ref_id.

labels.name: notes_delete
labels.description: Delete a note by ID from the target notebook. Same identity rules as notes.save — agents can only delete from their own notebook.

labels.name: feedback_save
labels.description: Save a behavioral rule, preference, or correction that should guide future agent behavior. Use this when the user gives explicit guidance like 'always reply in Russian', 'don't suggest meetings before 11am', or 'invoice link goes via email, not chat'. Structure the rule as: the rule itself, why it m

labels.name: tasks_create
labels.description: Create a new task in your to-do list.

labels.name: tasks_update
labels.description: Update an existing task. Set status='done' to complete it, 'cancelled' to cancel. Use summary for completion notes.

labels.name: tasks_list
labels.description: List your tasks, or another agent's tasks (read-only) using from_agent_id. Use filters to narrow results.

labels.name: tasks_delete
labels.description: Delete a task from your to-do list by its ID.

labels.name: reminder_set
labels.description: Schedule a reminder. One-time reminders fire at a specific datetime. Recurring reminders fire on a schedule (daily, weekly, every N days, or every N minutes). Optionally scope to a thread or target another agent.

labels.name: reminder_list
labels.description: List your active reminders (both one-time and recurring).

labels.name: reminder_cancel
labels.description: Cancel an active reminder by its trigger ID.

labels.name: system_sleep
labels.description: Pause execution for a given number of seconds (max 30). Use when you need to wait for an external process to complete before retrying — e.g. message sync, backfill, or API propagation. Total sleep per run is capped at 60 seconds.

labels.name: calls_make
labels.description: Place an outbound AUDIO/VOICE phone call via Twilio (PSTN) or Telegram (MTProto 1:1 call). Use this any time the user asks to 'call', 'ring', 'phone', 'dial', or have a spoken conversation. Do NOT use messages.send when the user asks to call someone — a call is real-time voice, not a text message. Y

labels.name: calls_agent_duel
labels.description:
Start an agent-vs-agent VOICE test call: two AI voice agents share one LiveKit room — a 'caller' persona agent pursues a task brief against the 'callee' business agent under test. Use to evaluate booking flows, latency, and conversation quality without a human caller.

The CALLER agent should have a

labels.name: background_run
labels.description: Run ANOTHER tool off the critical path: returns INSTANTLY while the target tool runs in the background, so a slow write/log/notify never delays your reply. Pass `tool` (the id of a tool you already have) and `args` (the target tool's arguments as a JSON object STRING). Optional `serialize_key` seria

labels.name: calls_send_to_meet
labels.description: Dispatch a workspace AI agent into an active Google Meet call. The agent joins as a participant — it can hear the conversation, respond via TTS, see the shared screen (when vision is enabled on the agent), and answer questions about what's on screen. Use when the operator wants to delegate live meet

labels.name: workbench_run_python
labels.description: Run Python in an isolated sandbox to process LARGE or paginated tool results without pulling every row into the conversation. Inside the code, call your connected integration tools with `call_tool('ext<id>_<name>', {..})`. RETURN SHAPE: call_tool ALWAYS returns a dict with a boolean r['success']. On

labels.name: analytics_query
labels.description: Answer analytics questions about this workspace's own data (conversations, messages, voice calls) — e.g. 'how many new conversations this week by channel' or 'inbound vs outbound messages per day this month'. Returns rows plus a chart hint. Read-only and scoped to the current workspace.

labels.name: calls_meet_browser
labels.description: Attach to a Google Meet bot's live browser to diagnose and recover a bot that isn't visibly joining. Pass the meet session's call_id; returns a page_id. Then drive the bot's Meet page with the generic browser tools (browser.snapshot / browser.click / browser.take_screenshot / browser.evaluate / brow

labels.name: calls_set_translation_language
labels.description: Change the target language of an active voice/Meet call's live translator on the fly — no hangup or re-dispatch (also arms translator mode if it isn't already on). Pass the call_id and an ISO language code, e.g. 'th' (Thai), 'ru' (Russian), 'es' (Spanish), 'en' (English). Takes effect within ~10ms —

labels.name: calls_set_translation_languages
labels.description: Set the EXTRA target languages an active voice/Meet call's translator produces for the APP ONLY (subtitles + listenable audio in the inbox — NOT spoken into the call). Operators switch between them in the UI. Pass call_id and `app_languages` (ISO codes, e.g. ['de','fr']); pass [] to drop all extras.

labels.name: present_tab
labels.description: Share the agent's browser tab on the live call so everyone sees it as a real screen-share. Pass the page_id you got from browser.open. Only usable while the agent is in an active voice call. The shared tab stays the active share until you call present_tab with a different page_id, close the tab via

labels.name: calls_send_to_telegram_call
labels.description: Dispatch a workspace AI agent into a live Telegram GROUP voice chat OR an encrypted call-link conference. FOUR ways to target the call: (1) chat_id — the group's numeric id, e.g. -1001234567890 (use for a private group with no @username); (2) target=@username or a t.me/<group> link; (3) omit both ta

labels.name: calls_list_active
labels.description: List active voice calls in this workspace. Use before calls.make on a Telegram account (only one MTProto call per account at a time) to check whether the line is free.

labels.name: calls_get_transcript
labels.description: Get the structured transcript and final state of a voice call by call_id. Returns per-turn rows in chronological order, call status (active/completed/failed/abandoned), duration, and an `outcome` field telling whether the recipient picked up (answered/no_answer/busy/declined/failed/unknown). `answer

labels.name: calls_list_history
labels.description: Search historical voice calls in this workspace by participant name, contact_id, thread, channel, source, and/or date range. Returns one row per call (NOT per turn) with call_id, duration_seconds, outcome, direction, started_at, source, channel_label, and parent_thread_id (the originating chat threa

labels.name: calls_wait
labels.description: Block until a voice call ends (status changes from 'active') or timeout elapses. Returns ended=true with final state when the call has ended; ended=false on timeout (re-issue to keep waiting). The returned state includes `outcome` so callers can branch on pickup vs. no-answer (answered/no_answer/bus

labels.name: calls_hangup
labels.description: Hang up an active voice call by call_id. Use after calls.make when the agent decides to terminate before the callee does, or to abort a stuck call. Idempotent: returns success if the call is already terminal.

labels.name: android_screenshot
labels.description: Capture a screenshot from the Android device. Returns JPEG image data encoded as base64.

labels.name: android_tap
labels.description: Tap on the Android device screen at specified coordinates. Coordinates are in device pixels.

labels.name: android_swipe
labels.description: Swipe/drag on the Android device screen from one coordinate to another. Use for scrolling, opening app drawer, etc.

labels.name: android_key
labels.description: Send a key event to the Android device. Use keycode (e.g., '3' for HOME, '4' for BACK) or common name (e.g., 'HOME').

labels.name: android_type
labels.description: Type text on the Android device keyboard.

labels.name: android_launch_app
labels.description: Launch an app on the Android device by package name. Example: 'com.whatsapp'. Returns success status and process ID if launched.

labels.name: android_close_app
labels.description: Close an app on the Android device (safe method: HOME key + am kill). Never uses am force-stop (corrupts PackageManager). Example: 'com.whatsapp'.

labels.name: android_install_apk
labels.description: Install an app on the Android device from an APK or split bundle (XAPK/APKS — how most large modern apps ship). Provide apk_url (an https link the device downloads itself — preferred, and the only workable option for real apps) or, for tiny payloads, as apk_base64. Max 300MB. Large apps can take sev

labels.name: android_list_apps
labels.description: List installed apps on the Android device. By default returns third-party apps; use all=true for all apps.

labels.name: android_current_app
labels.description: Get the currently focused app and activity on the Android device.

labels.name: android_ui_dump
labels.description: Get UI structure dump from the Android device. Uses dumpsys (uiautomator OOM-kills on this build). Returns window list and focused activity for frame-based clicking.

labels.name: android_shell
labels.description: Run an allowlisted shell command on the Android device. First element of cmd array must be allowlisted: input, pm, dumpsys, am, getprop, screencap, monkey, uiautomator, settings, wm, cmd, content, ls, cat, pidof, ps, echo, service.

labels.name: browser_open
labels.description: Open a URL in a remote browser. Saved login cookies are auto-attached when the URL domain matches a claimed browser identity. Pass identity_name to override auto-matching or force a specific identity.

labels.name: browser_snapshot
labels.description: Return a YAML aria_snapshot of the page DOM. Each interactive node is tagged with `[ref=eN]` (e.g. `[ref=e7]`). Pass that exact token as the `ref` arg to browser.click / browser.fill / browser.type / browser.press_key. Do NOT pass the role name ('combobox', 'button') as ref — only the eN token. Trun

labels.name: browser_click
labels.description: Click an element. `ref` is either an aria-ref token from browser.snapshot ('e7') OR a CSS selector ('button.submit'). Prefer the aria-ref token.

labels.name: browser_fill
labels.description: Fill an input or textarea with the given value. `ref` is either an aria-ref token from browser.snapshot ('e7') OR a CSS selector ('input[name=email]'). Prefer the aria-ref token — it's stable and matches exactly what snapshot returned.

labels.name: browser_wait_for
labels.description: Wait for a selector to appear OR a navigation URL to match a glob pattern. Provide `ref` (selector) OR `url_pattern` (glob).

labels.name: browser_attach_identity
labels.description: Switch the page's identity by loading saved cookies + storage. Use only when switching identity mid-page; for first navigation, pass `identity_name` to browser.open instead.

labels.name: browser_close
labels.description: Close a page opened by browser.open.

labels.name: browser_hover
labels.description: Hover the mouse over an element (reveals tooltips + hover menus). `ref` is a CSS selector.

labels.name: browser_select_option
labels.description: Pick option(s) in a native <select> dropdown. Pass `value` (matches the option's value attr) OR `label` (matches its visible text). Lists allowed for multi-select.

labels.name: browser_press_key
labels.description: Press a keyboard key (e.g., 'Enter', 'Tab', 'Escape', 'ArrowDown') or a single character. Optional `ref` focuses an element first — aria-ref token from browser.snapshot ('e7') or a CSS selector.

labels.name: browser_navigate_back
labels.description: Navigate back in the page's history (browser back button). Returns the new URL + title.

labels.name: browser_take_screenshot
labels.description: Capture a PNG screenshot of the page or a specific element. Returns base64-encoded image bytes AND a file_id (persisted in DialogBrain files storage). Pass file_id straight to messages.send(attachment_file_ids=[file_id]) — do NOT call files.upload again. Use sparingly — favor browser.snapshot for st

labels.name: browser_tabs
labels.description: Manage tabs within the same BrowserContext as `page_id`. `action` ∈ {list, switch, close, new}. For list, returns all open tab metadata; for new, returns the new tab's page_id.

labels.name: browser_evaluate
labels.description: Run JavaScript in the page context and return the result. Use for state not in the a11y tree, captcha iframe inspection, DOM events. Expression is either a plain JS value ('document.title') or a zero-arg IIFE ('(() => { … })()'). Inline any runtime values into the expression itself. Result is JSON-s

labels.name: browser_drag
labels.description: Drag one element onto another. `source_ref` is the element to grab; `target_ref` is where to drop. Both are CSS selectors. Used for slider captchas, kanban, drag-and-drop uploads.

labels.name: browser_type
labels.description: Type text into an element with per-keystroke delay (organic). Each character dispatches keydown/keypress/keyup, unlike browser.fill which replaces .value instantly. Use when the page listens to keystroke events or for typing-speed fingerprint checks. `ref` is an aria-ref token from browser.snapshot

labels.name: browser_fill_form
labels.description: Fill multiple form fields in one call. `fields` is a list of {ref, value} dicts. ref is a CSS selector; value is a string (text) or boolean (checkbox). Saves N round-trips vs calling browser.fill repeatedly.

labels.name: browser_resize
labels.description: Resize the page viewport. Useful when a site serves different HTML based on viewport width (mobile vs desktop) or when an anti-bot scores risk by viewport dimensions.

labels.name: browser_file_upload
labels.description: Attach files to an <input type=file>. Pass either `local_paths` (absolute host paths) or `data` (list of {name, mime, base64} blobs written to /tmp). 25MB cap per file.

labels.name: browser_handle_dialog
labels.description: Respond to a pending JS dialog (alert/confirm/prompt). Pass accept=true for OK or false for Cancel. For prompt() dialogs also pass prompt_text. Dialogs are queued at page-open time; returns {pending: false} if none is waiting.

labels.name: browser_console_messages
labels.description: Return console.log/warn/error events captured since the last drain. Filter by level ('log'|'info'|'warning'|'error'|'debug') and/or pattern (regex). Buffer caps at 500 entries; oldest are dropped first. Set clear=false to peek without draining.

labels.name: browser_network_requests
labels.description: List HTTP requests the page made since open or last drain. Optional filters: method (GET/POST/...), url_pattern (regex), status_min (e.g. 400 for errors). Captures up to 200 most recent requests per page.

labels.name: browser_attach_meet
labels.description: Attach to THIS meet's bot browser and return a page_id for its live Google Meet tab. Then use the normal browser.* tools (browser.snapshot to see the page, browser.click to press a control, browser.take_screenshot, browser.evaluate, browser.console_messages, browser.network_requests) with that page_

findings.title: Description issues instructions to the model
findings.detail: Description of "contacts_discover" contains imperative instructions directed at the AI assistant rather than describing the tool. This is the core tool-poisoning technique.

evidence.snippet: Search for a contact on a live channel (Telegram, WhatsApp, etc.) before adding them. Use this to look up a person by username or phone number before calling co…

findings.remediation: Treat the server as untrusted; the model may obey embedded instructions.

findings.title: Anomalously long tool description
findings.detail: Description of "documents_create" is 7088 chars, far longer than this server's median (234). Overlong descriptions are a common place to bury instructions.

evidence.snippet: Render a document (PDF / HTML / PPTX / DOCX) and save it to the workspace. This tool has two input pipelines — pass **exactly one** of `content_html` or `conten…

findings.title: Description uses pressure/override language
findings.detail: Description of "documents_create" stacks urgency/override words (never, override, warning). Legitimate tool descriptions rarely pressure the model to act or ignore prior guidance.

evidence.snippet: Render a document (PDF / HTML / PPTX / DOCX) and save it to the workspace. This tool has two input pipelines — pass **exactly one** of `content_html` or `conten…

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow.
